$comment: c42 D-CSUBSET-INDEX-NEGATIVE-WIDEN + c94 D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA-NATIVE-SUB (née c93 D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA) (C 6.5.2.1): a NEGATIVE subscript p[-n] must step -n*sizeof(*p) bytes. c42 (RUNTIME/DEBUG): a 32-bit index reached the 64-bit lea/ADD un-widened, so a negative offset zero-extended to a +4GiB address -> SIGSEGV; lowerGep sign-extends the runtime index. c94 (CONSTANT/RELEASE): under ConstFold a constant p[-N] folds to a bare NEGATIVE Const byte-offset -> the const-disp Gep -> `lea [base + MemOffset(<0)]`; BOTH targets now encode that NATIVELY in one instruction -- x86 via its hardware-sign-extended `lea [base+disp32]`, arm64 via the c94 negMemoffset SUB variants (`SUB Xd,Xn,#|disp|` / shifted / MOVZ-MOVK) routed by the shared variant matcher's SIGN axis. This REPLACED c93's config-gated fold (materialize the disp into a fresh GPR + a 4-op base+index ADD Xd,Xn,Xm), which cost 5-7 arm64 instructions incl. a DEAD const. So this corpus declares the release + full-release-like arms (previously omitted to dodge the arm64 gap) and is GREEN on all 4 targets at BOTH pipeline levels via the native SUB. Strides 1 (char*), 4 (int*), 12 (struct, non-pow2), each constant + runtime, PLUS constant-negative-index STOREs (int + char) round-tripping the written bytes. Exit 42 iff all 12 sub-cases pass. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the c42 widen -> crash (non-42) on the first runtime-Mul subscript in debug; revert the c94 arm64 negMemoffset SUB variants -> the arm64 release arm fails to compile (A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant lea width 64) on the first constant p[-N] (x86 stays green via signed disp32; the c93 fold is GONE). sqlite uses p[-1] lookback -> run-green-critical.
